title: 2026 F1 CHINESE GP Sprint Qualifying analysis by Peter Windsor
description: No surprise to see the Mercs on the front row again but McLaren-Mercedes are now right up there with Ferrari.  Lando Norris qualified P3 behind George Russell and Kimi Antonelli, ahead of Sir Lewis Hamilton (Ferrari), Oscar Piastri in the other McLaren-Mercedes, and Ferrari's Charles Leclerc. Pierre Gasly was an excellent P7 for Alpine-Mercedes, beating a dismayed Max Verstappen (Red Bull-RBPT Ford).  In this video, Peter looks at the key aspects of the day - particularly at the superb T16 corne

title: Dan Towriss: The ‘Passion & Drive’ Behind Cadillac F1 Team | F1 Beyond The Grid Podcast
description:
The newest team in Formula 1 are here to compete. Cadillac have earned the respect of their rivals. Now they’re pushing to get faster.

CEO Dan Towriss takes Tom Clarkson back to the start of the story - how he and his TWG Motorsports company first became involved in discussions about F1, conversations with General Motors and Cadillac, the work to build a team, choosing their race drivers and making their F1 debut in Australia.

Sergio Perez and Valtteri Bottas are the first Cadillac F1 drivers.

title: 2026 F1 Australian GP race analysis by Peter Windsor
description: The factory Mercedes team - George Russell heading Kimi Antonelli - duly finished one-two in Melbourne on Sunday, exhibiting more power, more downforce, more grip and more balance than all of their opposition - and capped all of that with perfect strategy calls. Ferrari pushed them hardest, with Charles Leclerc taking the lead into T1 and exchanging early-lap power surges with George Russell, but weird strategies quickly knocked them back to P3-P4, with Charles Leclerc crossing the line 0.6sec a

title: Australian grand prix Review | Day 10 of learning #F1 #drivetosurvive  #f1explained
description:
Ferrari… we have the car, we have the drivers… but strategy?”

What a crazy season opener at the Australian Grand Prix 🏁
George Russell wins the race, leading a Mercedes 1-2 with Kimi Antonelli, while Ferrari finishes P3 and P4 with Leclerc and Hamilton despite strong pace.

Meanwhile Max Verstappen climbed from P20 to P6, one of the drives of the race.

The 2026 F1 season has started with drama already.

Subscribe for daily F1 race analysis, strategy breakdowns, and Formula 1 explained.

description: It was a big day in Melbourne for the local hero, Oscar Piastri, heading, as he did, the FP2 times in Melbourne ahead of the two factory Mercedes drivers (Kimi Antonelli and George Russell).  Showing all the original style qualities of  the driver who burst into F1 in 2023, Oscar looked right at home in the new McLaren MCL40-Mercedes, proving not only to be quick through the demanding T9-T10 section but also, simultaneously, over the lap as a whole. title: New F1 fans get LOST watching races... this guide explains EVERYTHING in 60 seconds
description:
New to F1? This will save you on race day 🏁

If you’re a new Formula One fan, race broadcasts can look overwhelming. So many numbers. So many abbreviations. So much happening at once.

Relax. It’s way simpler than it looks.

At the very top, you’ll see the total number of laps and how many have been completed. That tells you how far into the race you are.

On the left side of the screen is the leaderboard. Each driver’s current position is shown next to their name. If someone overtakes, you’ll

title: F1 Drivers Explain F1

title: Carlos Sainz: Pushing For Progress With Williams | F1 Beyond The Grid Podcast
description:
Five years after Carlos Sainz left McLaren, his former team and his former teammate are the reigning Formula 1 World Champions. As he watched Lando Norris reach the summit of world motorsport, did he ever think ‘that could have been me?’.

Speaking to Tom Clarkson, Carlos reflects on leaving McLaren and becoming a race winner with Ferrari before joining Williams. He remembers feeling ‘powerless’ during a difficult start to the 2025 season, and reveals the work which led him to stand on the podiu

description:

Are Mercedes favourites? Are fast-starting Ferrari title contenders? How will McLaren’s teammate battle develop now Lando Norris is reigning champion? Can Max Verstappen put Red Bull in the mix? There are so many questions at the start of a new era of racing. Hinch, Jolyon and TC debate it all.

title: Juan Pablo Montoya's record-breaking lap of Monza | 2004 Italian GP
description:
Colombia's Juan Pablo Montoya averaged an eye-watering 259.83 km/h when setting his pole position lap around Monza back in 2004.

This stood as a record until 2018, when it was narrowly eclipsed by Kimi Raikkonen with an average speed of 263.587km/h: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q5_fo16E4LU

Lewis Hamilton then lowered the record further in 2020, with a lap that averaged 264.363 km/h: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2f1PtJV0vIs

Five years later, Max Verstappen went even faster, with an avera
